items.caption_text:
I remember when my wife told me she believed I was meant to become a billionaire and honestly, it irritated me.

From where I stood, I was already maxed out. I was grinding every day, sacrificing, pushing harder than anyone around me. I wasn’t coasting, I was all in. So when she said that, it didn’t feel like encouragement. It felt like more pressure. Like somehow, everything I was doing still wasn’t enough.

What I didn’t see at the time was her perspective.

She wasn’t questioning my work ethic. She wasn’t saying I needed to try harder. She was looking at my potential and realizing my targets were too small for the level of drive I had. And that’s a frustrating place to be, when your ambition is bigger than the goals you’ve given yourself. You end up working like crazy but still feeling like something’s missing.

Back then, I thought I was pushing my limits. In reality, I had just gotten comfortable with a version of them.

Elena wasn’t trying to make my life harder, she was trying to expand my vision. And that’s not always a comfortable experience. When someone sees more in you than you see in yourself, it forces you to confront the possibility that you’ve been playing smaller than you could.

That kind of push doesn’t always feel good in the moment. Sometimes it feels like friction. Sometimes it feels like pressure you don’t want. But looking back, I can see that it was necessary.

Because the truth is, when your capacity is high but your targets are low, that unused energy turns into frustration. You don’t feel fulfilled, you feel stuck, even though you’re working nonstop.

What I’ve learned is this, the strongest partnerships aren’t built on comfort. They’re built on growth. The right person in your corner won’t just support what you’re doing, they’ll challenge what you believe is possible.

And yeah, sometimes that’s annoying. Sometimes you resist it.

But if they really see you and they’re right, then that pressure you’re pushing against might be the exact thing you need to break through to the next level.

items.caption_text:
The people you surround yourself with aren’t just company, they’re influence. 

They shape your mindset, your standards, your habits, and ultimately your direction. Choose those who challenge you, support you, and inspire growth… because over time, their energy becomes yours.

items.caption_text:
6 years ago I was stripped of my name, my money, my contacts and was given $100, a truck, a phone with no numbers and 90 days to prove one thing: that success is strategy, not luck.

Most people think you need connections, followers, or investors to win. I proved that with the right plan and relentless execution, you can turn $100 into a $5.5 million business in 90 days.

I didn’t do Undercover Billionaire to prove to other people I could do it, in all honesty I did it for myself... so I KNEW I could do it. Because once you know you can accomplish so much with so little, you know you are qualified to share that information with the world and really help other people do the same thing in their own lives.

This wasn’t about being comfortable. It was about being committed. Winners don’t wait for perfect conditions, they create them. They take every challenge, turn it into a lever, and use it to amplify action.

This weekend I am hosting a free webinar to share my 6 key takeaways from this experience and how you can use them to really expand and dominate the rest of 2026.

And no, you don’t need to shave your head, change your name, and be stripped down to your last $100 for this to work. Trust me.

Comment FREE to claim your free access to my webinar on Sunday.

items.caption_text:
~88 profitable U.S. corporations paid $0 in federal corporate income tax while you paid payroll tax, income tax, property tax, sales tax, capital gain tax, etc to fund activities you don’t even support.  The big guys know how to Pay Nothing.  Wouldn’t you like to know how they do it?

Join me to learn what tax planners, wealth advisors and the super wealthy are doing to reduce their taxes.
